observation: I see a language selection dropdown is currently open, showing many language options. But I need to focus on the country selection for signup. Looking at the page, I can see there's a 'Country/region' dropdown with a 'Select' placeholder that I need to click first. There's also a red 'Next' button at the bottom. The page shows 'Select your residence' as the main heading, which confirms this is the country selection step.
reasoning: As an elderly user, I need to select my country of residence to continue with the signup process. I can see the country dropdown is not opened yet - it shows 'Select' as placeholder text. I should tap on this dropdown to open the country options and then select a suitable country.
ux_issues: Language dropdown is open and covering part of the interface, making it confusing to navigate
The country dropdown placeholder text 'Select' is quite small for elderly users

outcome.visual_observation: UX issues detected: Language dropdown is open and covering part of the interface, making it confusing to navigate, The country dropdown placeholder text 'Select' is quite small for elderly users
outcome.diagnosis: Analysis completed - see evidence for details

issues: Language dropdown is open and covering part of the interface, making it confusing to navigate
The country dropdown placeholder text 'Select' is quite small for elderly users

dual_diagnosis.visual_observation: The tap action on the Country/region input field successfully opened a country selection modal/overlay. The interface transitioned from showing a simple dropdown with 'Select' placeholder to displaying a full country selection interface with a search bar and a scrollable list of countries with flags (Guinea-Bissau, Guyana, Haiti, Honduras, Hungary, Iceland, India, Indonesia, Iraq, Ireland, Israel, Italy, Jamaica, Japan, Kazakhstan, etc.). A red circular highlight appears around the Guyana option, indicating user interaction capability.
